case_title: LYNETTE BROUSSARD VS. URBAN ALCHEMY ET AL

DECLARATION OF DANIEL B HENDERSON IN SUPPORT OF EX PARTE APPLICATION CONTINUING TRIAL AND CONTINUING ALL CUTOFF DATES WITH THE NEW TRIAL DATE IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE PARTIES STIPULATION (TRANSACTION ID # 75634537) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BROUSSARD, LYNETTE

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO JUNE-05-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT URBAN ALCHEMY'S MOTION TO COMPEL NEUROPSYCHOLOGICAL AND PSYCHIATRIC EXAMINATIONS OF PLAINTIFF; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATIONS. THE COURT ORDERS THE REQUESTED NEUROPSYCHOLOGICAL AND PSYCHIATRIC EXAMINATIONS WITHOUT DISCLOSURE OF RAW DATA TO BROUSSARD'S COUNSEL, BUT URBAN ALCHEMY'S EXPERTS MUST DISCLOSE THE RAW DATA TO BROUSSARD'S RETAINED NEUROPSYCHOLOGICAL EXPERT (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, WITH A COVER LETTER DEMONSTRATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Neuropsychological And Pyschiatric Examinations Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jun-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Apperances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DANIEL HENDERSON, Esq. of The Henderson Firm, P.C. (tel: 323-487-0193) for Plaintiff Lynette Broussard.
Atty. JASMYNE SHAW, Esq. of Freeman Mathis & Gary, LLP (tel: 415-394-9500) for Defendant Urban Alchemy.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT URBAN ALCHEMY's Motion To Compel Neuropsychological And Psychiatric Examinations Of Plaintiff.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications.

Exercising its discretion, the court orders the requested neuropsychological and psychiatric examinations without disclosure of raw data to Broussard's counsel, but Urban Alchemy's experts must disclose the raw data to Broussard's retained neuropsychological expert (see word minutes for complete ruling).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
